meta.owner: UNASSIGNED — name a person/role responsible for keeping these current (Phase 0, assumptions ownership)
meta.description: Single source of truth for HelioScout scoring thresholds and financial assumptions. The backend scoring engine and the frontend financial/reconciliation engines both read from this file. Update values, sources and asOf dates HERE only — the per-deploy copies (backend/assumptions.json, frontend/data/assumptions.json) are generated from this file. Any value whose source reads 'UNSOURCED' has not been independently verified and must be confirmed by the assumptions owner before official use.

_basis.solarGHIBands: GHI bands for utility-scale PV siting. Libya's high-resource zones exceed 6 kWh/m²/day GHI (Global Solar Atlas / NASA POWER). Band cut-offs are a project convention calibrated to that resource range; treat as a siting heuristic, not a standard.
_basis.windV100Bands: Hub-height (100 m) mean wind speed mapped to IEC 61400-1 wind turbine classes (Class I ~10 m/s ref, down to Class IV). ~6 m/s is the commonly cited floor for commercial onshore viability.
_basis.cspDNIBands: Annual DNI viability bands for CSP. ~2000 kWh/m²/yr is the widely cited minimum for bankable CSP and ~2500+ kWh/m²/yr is considered excellent (IRENA / NREL / SolarPACES CSP resource guidance).
_basis.tempImpact: Heuristic PV performance derate flags for warm climates (module efficiency falls with cell temperature). Indicative only.
_basis.shear: Default 1/7 power-law exponent (α=0.143) for open terrain; clamped to 0.10–0.40 when derived from the NASA 10 m→50 m ratio.

discountRate.source: UNSOURCED — assumed real discount rate / WACC. Owner to confirm an appropriate rate for Libyan public-sector renewable investment.

solar.source: Indicative utility-scale PV cost (order-of-magnitude, IRENA Renewable Power Generation Costs / NREL ATB range). Owner to confirm for Libya.

wind.source: Indicative onshore wind cost (IRENA / NREL ATB range). Owner to confirm for Libya.

csp.source: Indicative CSP (tower/trough) cost (IRENA / NREL ATB range). Owner to confirm for Libya.

co2FactorKgPerMMBtu.source: US EPA GHG Emission Factors Hub — natural gas combustion (~53.06 kg CO2 / MMBtu, HHV).

derating.source: Engineering convention for gas-turbine heat-rate derating: ~0.35%/°C above 15°C ISO ambient; ~0.3%/yr vintage degradation for the first 10 years then ~0.5%/yr, capped at 15%. Indicative — owner to confirm against fleet data.

financial._turbinesSource: Manufacturer ISO-condition heat rates / efficiencies (indicative, public datasheets). Representative of North-African gas fleets. Owner/GECOL to validate the class→turbine mapping (Phase 4 / W7).

domestic.source: UNSOURCED — administered/subsidised domestic gas price (USD/MMBtu). Owner to confirm current figure and as-of date.

export.source: UNSOURCED — export-parity gas price (USD/MMBtu). This is the figure NOC will scrutinise hardest; owner to set a current, dated, sourced value.

gasPrices._sensitivityBasis: Fractional band applied to the chosen export gas price to produce a low/expected/high NPV range (±25%). Government investment decisions need ranges, not point estimates; gas price is the dominant NPV driver.

landPowerDensityMWPerKm2.source: Utility-scale fixed-tilt PV total-area land use (~7 acres/MW ⇒ ~35 MW/km²), NREL 'Land-Use Requirements for Solar Power Plants in the United States' (TP-6A20-56290). Owner to confirm packing factor for Libyan sites.

transmission.source: UNSOURCED — indicative greenfield HV overhead line cost (USD/km) by voltage and a fixed substation-bay interconnection cost, flat/desert terrain (order-of-magnitude, World Bank / utility transmission-planning ranges). Owner to confirm current figures for Libya/GECOL.

populationFeasibility.source: Heuristic land/space-constraint bands applied to the modelled population-density field: above the infeasible threshold a site is treated as built-up with no contiguous open land for a utility-scale farm; the constrained band flags peri-urban siting risk. Owner to calibrate against land-cover data.

nationalBcf.source: Order-of-magnitude anchor from EIA Libya country analysis / Statistical Review of World Energy: Libyan power generation is ~99% gas-fired and gas consumption is dominated by the power sector. Tunable reference, not a precise published line item.

reconciliation._basis: Bottom-up fleet gas estimate (per-plant derated heat rate × capacity × 8760 h × utilisation) is reconciled against nationalBcf. Validated when the ratio falls within validationBand (±25%). Natural-gas energy content ~1,037 Btu/cf (HHV) → 1 Bcf ≈ 1,037,000 MMBtu.
